sse:
event: content_block_delta
data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"Key facts confirmed:
- `run()` and `step()` return `unknown` — the compiler flows the thunk's inferred return type through, so I don't need to cast.
- `stepWithTimeout` returns a `Result` — must check `.ok`.
- A handler using `zttp:durable` (write effect) **cannot** hold `read_only`/`idempotent`/`retry_safe`, so I must declare a narrow `Spec<...>` on the return type.
- No ternary, no compound assignment, no `as` casts — canonical style throughout.

Now let me forge the handler with the proof-first route and simulate it before writing:"}}

event: content_block_delta
data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"file\": \"handler.ts\", \"content\": \"// Durable order workflow handler\\n// Two-step pipeline: reserve → charge, crash-safe via zttp:durable run/step.\\n\\nimport { run, step } from \\\"zttp:durable\\\";\\nimport { decodeJson } from \\\"zttp:decode\\\";\\nimport { schemaCompile } from \\\"zttp:validate\\\";\\nimport { logInfo, logError } from \\\"zttp:log\\\";\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Request schema – compiled once at module load\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nschemaCompile(\\\"order\\\", JSON.stringify({\\n    type: \\\"object\\\",\\n    required: [\\\"orderId\\\", \\\"userId\\\", \\\"amount\\\"],\\n    properties: {\\n        orderId: { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n        userId:  { type: \\\"string\\\", minLength: 1 },\\n        amount:  { type: \\\"number\\\", minimum: 1 },\\n    },\\n}));\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Step helpers – pure business functions, no I/O of their own\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ntype OrderInput = {\\n    orderId: string;\\n    userId:  string;\\n    amount:  number;\\n};\\n\\ntype ReservationResult = {\\n    reservationId: string;\\n    reserved:      boolean;\\n};\\n\\ntype ChargeResult = {\\n    chargeId:   string;\\n    charged:    boolean;\\n    amount:     number;\\n};\\n\\nfunction buildReservation(order: OrderInput): ReservationResult {\\n    // Deterministic reservation record derived from the input.\\n    // In production this step would call an inventory service via fetchSync.\\n    return {\\n        reservationId: \\\"rsv_\\\" + order.orderId,\\n        reserved:      true,\\n    };\\n}\\n\\nfunction buildCharge(order: OrderInput, reservation: ReservationResult): ChargeResult {\\n    // Deterministic charge record.\\n    // In production this step would call a payment service via fetchSync.\\n    return {\\n        chargeId: \\\"chg_\\\" + reservation.reservationId,\\n        charged:  true,\\n        amount:   order.amount,\\n    };\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Handler\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction handler(req: Request): Response & Spec<\\\"deterministic\\\" | \\\"injection_safe\\\" | \\\"no_secret_leakage\\\" | \\\"no_credential_leakage\\\" | \\\"input_validated\\\"> {\\n    if (req.method !== \\\"POST\\\") {\\n        return Response.json({ error: \\\"method not allowed\\\" }, { status: 405 });\\n    }\\n\\n    // Validate + decode request body against the compiled schema.\\n    const bodyRaw = req.body ?? \\\"\\\";\\n    const parsed = decodeJson(\\\"order\\\", bodyRaw);\\n    if (!parsed.ok) {\\n        return Response.json({ errors: parsed.errors }, { status: 400 });\\n    }\\n\\n    const order = parsed.value;\\n    const orderId = order.orderId;\\n\\n    logInfo(\\\"order.received\\\", { orderId: orderId, userId: order.userId });\\n\\n    // run() is the durable envelope: if the process crashes mid-flight,\\n    // the runtime replays from the last committed step checkpoint.\\n    const result = run(\\\"order-\\\" + orderId, () => {\\n        // Step 1 – Reserve inventory.\\n        // step() records the return value; on replay it is returned instantly\\n        // without re-executing the thunk.\\n        const reservation = step(\\\"reserve\\\", () => buildReservation(order));\\n\\n        if (!reservation.reserved) {\\n            logError(\\\"order.reserve.failed\\\", { orderId: orderId });\\n            return Response.json(\\n                { error: \\\"reservation failed\\\", orderId: orderId },\\n                { status: 409 },\\n            );\\n        }\\n\\n        logInfo(\\\"order.reserved\\\", { reservationId: reservation.reservationId });\\n\\n        // Step 2 – Charge the customer.\\n        const charge = step(\\\"charge\\\", () => buildCharge(order, reservation));\\n\\n        if (!charge.charged) {\\n            logError(\\\"order.charge.failed\\\", { orderId: orderId, reservationId: reservation.reservationId });\\n            return Response.json(\\n                { error: \\\"charge failed\\\", orderId: orderId },\\n                { status: 402 },\\n            );\\n        }\\n\\n        logInfo(\\\"order.charged\\\", { chargeId: charge.chargeId, amount: charge.amount });\\n\\n        return Response.json({\\n            ok:            true,\\n            orderId:       orderId,\\n            reservationId: reservation.reservationId,\\n            chargeId:      charge.chargeId,\\n            amount:        charge.amount,\\n        }, { status: 201 });\\n    });\\n\\n    return result;\\n}\\n\"}"}}
